mnemonics.title: Mnemonic 1
mnemonics.content: Each Column's Sum = 1 (ECS1): A stochastic matrix's columns must sum to 1, representing total probability outflow from a state.

mnemonics.title: Mnemonic 2
mnemonics.content: Positive People Make Progress: All entries in a stochastic matrix are non-negative (≥0), and they represent probabilities (P).

mnemonics.title: Key Point 1
mnemonics.content: A stochastic matrix is a square matrix where each column sums to 1 and all entries are non-negative.

mnemonics.title: Key Point 2
mnemonics.content: Stochastic matrices are used to represent the transition probabilities of a Markov chain, where the (i, j) entry is the probability of moving from state j to state i.

mnemonics.title: Key Point 3
mnemonics.content: The stationary distribution of a Markov chain is a probability vector that remains unchanged after application of the transition matrix, satisfying π = πP.
